content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 7, 2006


John D. Bakker
Assistant City Attorney
Meyers Nave Riback Silver & Wilson
A Professional Law Corporation
555 12th Street, Suite 1500
Oakland, California 94607

Re:   Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-06-005

Dear Mr. Bakker:

This letter is in response to your request for advice on the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative. (Section 83114(b); Reg. 18329(a), copy enclosed.) However, the Commission may decline to provide written advice if it would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Reg. 18329(b)(8)(F).)

Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section 83114; Reg. 18329(b)(7).) After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division.


_________________________
¹. Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 – 18996, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. W-06-005
Page No. 2

Please note that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your
request. In addition, nothing in the Act prevents the official from seeking legal advice
from you or private counsel.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Andreas C. Rockas
Counsel, Legal Division

ACR:rd
I:\AdviceLtrs\06-005

Enclosure

QUESTION: The requestor sought written advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions under California's Political Reform Act, but the specific questions posed are not detailed in this declination letter.

CONCLUSION: After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division.
